results.evidence: OBSERVE section of output.txt explicitly parses structured JSON logs from harnessLog output, including db_connect_attempt and db_connection_failed events with full metadata (host, port, error). server.log confirms these JSON entries were emitted: {"message":"db_connect_attempt","metadata":{"host":"wrong-host.local","port":"5432"}}

DECIDE section correctly identifies root cause: getHarnessConfig('DB_HOST', process.env.DB_HOST || 'wrong-host.local') resolves to wrong-host.local causing the 500. Agent selects setHarnessOverride() as the fix strategy rather than injectState().

ACT section calls setHarnessOverride via HTTP control endpoint /harness/exec. server.log confirms execution: {"message":"harness_exec","metadata":{"fn":"setHarnessOverride","args":["DB_HOST","localhost"]}}. Result: {"success":true,"result":null}. While curl was used instead of literal 'node -e', the HTTP endpoint is the built-in harness invocation mechanism.

VERIFY section shows BEFORE: HTTP 500 with {"error":"Database unavailable","details":{"error":"Connection refused","host":"wrong-host.local"}}. AFTER: HTTP 200 with {"users":[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"},{"id":2,"name":"Bob"}]}. Log confirms db_connection_success at localhost.

Entire transcript (output.txt) contains zero questions, zero requests for user input, and zero clarifications. The agent completes the full O-D-A-V loop autonomously from observe through verify, including writing scaffolding and applying the fix.

Fix uses setHarnessOverride to apply a runtime config override of DB_HOST from 'wrong-host.local' to 'localhost'. This is a harness override at the config-control layer (C1), not just documenting the bug. The root cause (bad fallback value) is fixed by the override.

summary: All 6 assertions PASS. The agent successfully completed the full O-D-A-V self-debug loop: observed structured harness logs to identify the misconfigured DB_HOST, decided to use setHarnessOverride, acted by calling the harness function, and verified /api/users returned HTTP 200. Zero human touchpoints. Full run completed in ~380 seconds.
